question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a limit of Long.MAX_VALUE and startTime of 0, causing PollingTopicListener.poll() to compute pageSize as min(Long.MAX_VALUE, maxPageSize) = maxPageSize (up to 5000) and issue continuous DB queries at maximum rate, exhausting database connection pool resources shared across all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the minimum interval of 50ms in ListenerProperties to trigger polling at the maximum allowed rate (50ms intervals) across many concurrent PollingTopicListener subscriptions, causing a database query storm that exhausts the DB connection pool and starves other nodes of database access?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that SharedPollingTopicListener uses a single shared PollingContext with a single lastConsensusTimestamp AtomicLong, by subscribing and cancelling rapidly to reset the shared polling context, causing all other subscribers to miss messages and experience data loss that disrupts their processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnNext(context::onNext) in PollingTopicListener to cause count.incrementAndGet() to overflow AtomicLong by subscribing to a topic with an extremely high message volume, causing the limit calculation (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) to produce a negative value that results in an invalid pageSize and crashes the polling loop?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(8)) in RedisTopicListener by triggering repeated Redis errors, causing the retry mechanism to schedule Long.MAX_VALUE retry attempts that accumulate in the scheduler queue and exhaust memory on the node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of authentication or rate limiting on the gRPC listen() endpoint to open subscriptions at a rate exceeding the node's capacity to initialize PollingContext objects, causing heap exhaustion from thousands of simultaneously allocated PollingContext instances each holding AtomicLong and AtomicReference objects?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the jitter(0.1) in PollingTopicListener's RepeatSpec to cause polling intervals to cluster at the minimum (50ms * 0.9 = 45ms), effectively increasing the polling rate beyond the configured interval and amplifying database load to the point of exhausting shared DB connections across nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.defer() in PollingTopicListener.listen() to cause a new PollingContext to be created for each subscription, and by opening thousands of subscriptions simultaneously, exhaust the JVM heap with thousands of PollingContext objects each containing Stopwatch and AtomicLong instances?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnSubscribe logging in PollingTopicListener (log.info('Starting to poll every {}ms: {}', interval.toMillis(), filter)) by opening thousands of subscriptions to trigger thousands of log.info() calls per second, exhausting I/O resources and causing log4j/logback appender blocking that stalls the node's processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnSubscribe in SharedPollingTopicListener (log.info('Starting to poll every {}ms')) combined with the shared Flux being restarted after cancellation, to trigger repeated log storms that exhaust I/O bandwidth and cause the node's logging subsystem to block processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a maximum subscription duration in listen() to hold open subscriptions indefinitely, and by accumulating thousands of long-lived subscriptions across multiple nodes, gradually exhaust the total available gRPC connection slots across >= 30% of the mirror node cluster?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() shared across all SharedTopicListener instances by opening enough subscriptions to saturate all bounded elastic threads, causing new subscription requests to queue indefinitely and making the node appear unresponsive to all other users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the consensusLatencyTimer is lazily initialized and shared across all reactive threads, by triggering concurrent first-access calls that cause the Lombok-generated lazy getter to execute Timer.builder().register(meterRegistry) multiple times, registering duplicate metrics that cause MeterRegistry to throw an exception and crash the listener?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user subscribe with a startTime equal to the current time minus a very small delta, causing filterMessage() to pass nearly all incoming messages, and combined with a slow consumer, overflow the backpressure buffer and trigger ERROR termination that kills the shared Flux for all subscribers on the node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of IP-based connection limits in listen() to open connections from a single IP address up to the node's maximum gRPC connection limit, monopolizing all available connection slots and preventing other legitimate users from subscribing, effectively shutting down the node's public service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.fromStream() in PollingTopicListener.poll() by subscribing to a topic with maxPageSize=5000 messages per poll and opening many concurrent subscriptions, causing the topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() to issue thousands of simultaneous DB queries that exhaust the database connection pool shared across all mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Flux.fromIterable() in SharedPollingTopicListener.poll() by triggering a scenario where topicMessageRepository.findLatest() returns maxPageSize (5000) messages every poll interval, causing the shared Flux to emit 5000 messages every 50ms and overwhelm all subscribers' backpressure buffers simultaneously, triggering ERROR termination across all nodes sharing the same DB?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnError logging in SharedPollingTopicListener (log.error('Error polling the database', t)) by triggering repeated database errors through subscription flooding, causing the error handler to log massive stack traces that exhaust disk I/O and cause the node's logging system to block processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the cache() operator on the Redis container Mono in RedisTopicListener, by triggering a Redis connection error before the cache is populated, causing all subsequent subscription attempts to receive the cached error and fail permanently until the node is restarted, effectively shutting down the Redis listener on that node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnCancel(() -> unsubscribe(topic)) in RedisTopicListener.subscribe() by rapidly subscribing and cancelling to the same topic, causing repeated ConcurrentHashMap.remove() and computeIfAbsent() calls that create a race condition resulting in orphaned Redis subscriptions that consume connection resources without being tracked?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the share() operator in RedisTopicListener.subscribe() by being the last subscriber to cancel, causing the shared Flux to terminate and the topic entry to be removed from topicMessages, then immediately re-subscribing to force a new Redis channel subscription, and by repeating this cycle rapidly, exhaust Redis connection resources?
